name: jolt-rs-task-list
description: Port the Jolt framework from Zig to Rust, phase by phase, with fine-grained task breakdown.
source: Generated from spec_rust.md on 2026-05-10. Broken down into small, independently-verifiable tasks.
problem_statement: Port ~10k lines of Jolt (Zig) to a Rust workspace crate-for-crate, preserving endpoint/middleware/WS/pubsub/typegen behavior while replacing C dependencies with pure-Rust equivalents.
scope: Workspace of 5 crates + binary: jolt-core, jolt-macros, jolt-db, jolt-utils, jolt-templates, jolt-types.
Axum + tower + tokio for HTTP, WS, SSE, tasks, pub/sub.
sqlx for async DB with compile-time query checking and migrations.
Proc-macro-driven endpoint registration, auto-middleware, patch queries, and TypeScript typegen.
Replacement of facil.io (C) pub/sub with tokio::sync::broadcast + DashMap.
TLS via axum-server + rustls, file serving via tower-http.
Full example application exercising endpoints, WS, tasks, and DB migrations.
non_goals: Exact line-for-line behavioral parity where Zig semantics don't map to Rust.
C FFI compatibility or wrapping Zig libraries.
Zero-copy optimizations that complicate the initial port.
product_decisions: Middleware short-circuits via a RequestExt extension flag (req.markAsFinished → finished: AtomicBool).
Optional<T> is a tri-state enum: Some(T), Null (explicit JSON null), NotProvided (field absent).
WebSocket auth extracts JWT from Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header before upgrade.
Pub/sub maps to tokio::sync::broadcast channels keyed by name in a DashMap.
TypeScript typegen is a proc-macro side effect, opt-in via a jolt-types binary.
TLS uses rustls (no OpenSSL/LibreSSL C deps).

steps: Concrete step
verification: Command or observable check
